title: Mega Lopunny Battle Guide
summary: A 136 Atk, 135 Speed [Normal]/[Fighting] physical [sweeper] whose [scrappy] lets [return] and [fake-out] punch straight through [Ghost] immunities.
strengths: Base 135 Speed outspeeds almost everything in the unboosted tier without any setup
[return] plus [close-combat] plus [triple-axel] covers 90% of the metagame
weaknesses: 65/94/96 bulk folds to super-effective [Psychic], [Fairy], or [Flying] hits
No native setup move makes finishing a sweep harder than it looks
[close-combat] drops Def and SpDef by one stage each, opening [revenge]-kill windows

recommendedMoves.reason: is the STAB [Normal] main that hits [Ghost]s thanks to [scrappy]
is the [Fighting] STAB that pastes [Rock], [Steel], and [Ice]
provides the turn-one flinch Mega Lopunny needs to start sweeps

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Lopunny is the fast version of the base form's plan: 135 Speed and 136 Atk in the [Normal]/[Fighting] combination, with [scrappy] installed to make the whole set ignore [Ghost] immunities. It is one of the few [sweeper]s in the format that does not need a setup move to start a clean — the raw speed tier and STAB cove[rage] carry it by themselves.

## Strengths
STAB [return] plus [scrappy] is the unique selling point — no other [Normal] attacker can reliably threaten [Ghost] [pivot]s for full damage. [close-combat] shreds [Rock], [Steel], and [Ice] walls that would [normal]ly [absorb] [return], leaving [Dragon]s and Fairies as the only common resists. 135 Speed matches the Scarf tier of most offensive teams, so Mega Lopunny wins speed races even without a booster. [fake-out] gives it an on-demand tempo turn that most [sweeper]s lack.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Defenses at 65/94/96 look workable but fold quickly to any super-effective hit — [Psychic], [Fairy], and [Flying] [revenge]-killers end games fast if they come in cleanly. [close-combat] drops Def and SpDef one stage each, which hands back a free [revenge]-kill window to oppo[sing] priority users. The lack of a native setup move means Mega Lopunny has no way to turn one snowball turn into a guaranteed sweep — every KO has to be earned on raw stats.

## Recommended Moveset
[return] is the [scrappy]-defining main STAB and the [sing]le most important slot on the set. [close-combat] handles the [Rock], [Steel], and [Ice] walls that would otherwise resist [return]. [fake-out] is the tempo tool that buys the first real kill and needs to be kept available for the early game. [triple-axel] is the cove[rage] of choice for [Dragon], [Flying], and [Ground], accepting the 90% accuracy risk because the alternative moves trade away too much cove[rage]. [drain-punch] is a legitimate [substitute] if you want more sustain at the cost of wallbreaking ceiling.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[lopunnite] is the only legal item — [scrappy] and the stat shift are locked behind Mega Evolution. For Tera, [Fighting] is the primary option because it makes [close-combat] the strongest move on the set and partially mitigates [Fairy] matchups, while Tera [Normal] is the alternative when your plan is [return]-based finishing against [Ghost]-heavy teams.

## Teammates & Synergy
Bring [Fairy] and [Psychic] answers behind Mega Lopunny — a [Dark]-type partner or a [Poison] type covers the two matchups Mega Lopunny cannot break. A [stealth-rock] remover keeps Mega entry cost down, and a slow [pivot] on your team lets [fake-out] land on a softer target. A late-game cleaner completes the core, since Mega Lopunny tends to leave threats at 20-40% HP rather than scoring clean sweeps on its own.

## Play Patterns
Mega Evolve on the entry turn — the 135 Speed tier is the whole plan. Press [fake-out] immediately for tempo and then branch: [close-combat] into [Rock], [Steel], or [Ice], [return] into anything else, and [triple-axel] only against pure [Dragon] or [Flying] walls. Avoid c[lick]ing [close-combat] back-to-back; the defense drops com[pound] and invite priority [revenge] kills.
